title: Post EoE Yui Memorial or Mother split
display_title: Post EoE Yui Memorial or Mother split
wikitext:
[]: Post-EoE Yui: Memorial, or Mother? [split]             Quote Post by Atropos » Sun Sep 14, 2014 9:17 am [Split from the Canon Tiers topic. - Monk] Bagheera wrote: I believe it does, since it sheds significant light on both Yui's and Seele's motivations and methodology.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Sep 14, 2014 9:42 am Atropos wrote: We already knew what they were doing. Yui wanted to leave behind a memorial to humanity that would last forever, and Seele wanted to unite all human souls in order to end the pain of the human condition. That much, we learned from the 26 episodes of Neon Genesis Evangelion and the followup movies. Isn't that enough?

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sun Sep 14, 2014 9:55 am Bagheera wrote: No, because it's stupid . Particularly in Yui's case. But if her "monument to humanity" is a reference to the FAR, and not the Lilin, and this is just a poetic way of saying she's carrying out their original directive, it takes on a whole new spin. Highbrow SF and still arguably pretty dumb, but at least there's some principle involved.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Sep 14, 2014 10:12 am Sorrow wrote: Well, I don't think becoming a monument to humanity (the Lilin) is particularly stupid. If everything is derived from the first ancestral race, then there are already many monuments to them. The Lilin being a unique branch, from a unique seed, would give her more than enough reason to say "we were here".

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Sun Sep 14, 2014 10:37 am Bagheera wrote: That's not a good reason to wreck the world, abandon your family, and condemn yourself to the madness of loneliness for aeons, no. That's a reason to build a monument, nothing more.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 14, 2014 11:05 am Since I'm pretty sure I converted Bags some time ago, I think he's alluding to this: Floating around in space forever with the hope that somebody notices you and wonders how you got there is kind of stupid when NGE's mythos includes a rather more proactive way of immortalizing one's species. The FAR created entities capable of surviving long intergalactic journeys who would eventually colonize worlds with new life. This is quite clever, since the Seeds live for a really long time and they can provide an audience capable of wondering, "Where did I come from?" Yui appears to have had access to the SDSS and would have known about the FAR's deeds. But if she read that stuff and decided, "Hmm, I could repeat history and immortalize my species by inhabiting a copy of a Seed, getting the right power-ups, and heading off this rock for a younger one where I could put this body to good use, but you know what? I think I'd rather float around in outer space marinating in my own misery and loneliness forever", then, yes, I'd say she is as mad as everyone says. But the "Yui is insane" card just feels lazy and unsatisfying. Becoming a mother again suits her character better than becoming a mere monument, besides.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sun Sep 14, 2014 12:16 pm Reichu wrote: "Hmm, I could repeat history and immortalize my species by inhabiting a copy of a Seed, getting the right power-ups, and heading off this rock for a younger one where I could put this body to good use, but you know what? I think I'd rather float around in outer space marinating in my own misery and loneliness forever", then, yes, I'd say she is as mad as everyone says. But the "Yui is insane" card just feels lazy and unsatisfying.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Sep 14, 2014 12:56 pm Sorrow, Reichu just explained why that line of reasoning is not compelling. I believe her take on the matter jibes better than any other with both what we see in the show and what we see in the supplementary material. But that is a discussion perhaps more appropriate for the FAR thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sun Sep 14, 2014 1:06 pm Bagheera wrote: Sorrow, Reichu just explained why that line of reasoning is not compelling. I believe her take on the matter jibes better than any other with both what we see in the show and what we see in the supplementary material. But that is a discussion perhaps more appropriate for the FAR thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Sep 14, 2014 1:25 pm Sorrow wrote: Reichu explaining why something doesn't seem compelling to herself or you doesn't not mean it isn't compelling for myself or someone else. I'm afraid you saying that or Reichu saying her bit isn't enough to compel me.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Sep 14, 2014 1:34 pm Any idea of nobility behind Yui's actions is negated by what she left behind.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sun Sep 14, 2014 1:40 pm Bagheera wrote: The problem is that your position relies on the notion of a legacy, which only has meaning because people are around to appreciate it. Dooming your world to create such a legacy that no one will ever see is the very height of irrationality. It isn't a subjective matter -- the logic simply doesn't work.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 14, 2014 1:44 pm Sorrow wrote: Though this is assuming an awful lot about what Yui can actually achieve in her new form.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sun Sep 14, 2014 1:56 pm Reichu wrote: If cloning a Seed of Life and stuffing a soul and both Fruits into it doesn't produce another viable Seed, what in the world would?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Sep 14, 2014 2:09 pm Sorrow wrote: It isn't my position, I have yet to decide what exactly I think her end goal of becoming a god is to achieve. I'll argue either side until I'm convinced of one, or another, myself. However, you're assuming that nothing will be around to notice is as pointless as I may assume something does notice - both are beside the point. The point is that humanity (in the form of Yui) will out last everything else. If the point of living, breeding, evolving and creating better living conditions, is to ensure survival for as long as possible, then Yui has found a way to win the game of life. It doesn't matter if other life isn't there to take note of it. It is a selfless and yet rather selfish act. It's rather profound and important.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 14, 2014 2:13 pm Sorrow wrote: If we were shown her having something like the ability to create infinite amounts of LCL, then it would be a little more clear cut.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sun Sep 14, 2014 2:37 pm Bagheera wrote: No, it's not. She's in the middle of deep space and has nothing to do. It couldn't be less important.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Sep 14, 2014 2:48 pm Sorrow wrote: Selfishness. You deny the discussion your opinion because you wish to save it for your personal works!

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sun Sep 14, 2014 3:03 pm Bagheera wrote: How is that selfish? Nothing's stopping you from discussing them in the appropriate venue. I've also addressed it directly in this thread .

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 14, 2014 3:36 pm Sorrow: The Seeds' "special ingredient" might just be the way that each one is connected to a Chamber of Guf, i.e. a pocket of space that houses a supply of souls, or something. And Eva-01 does seem to have the ability to manifest one. First of all, in episode 25' and 26', Eva-01 is referred to in the capacity of an "ark", the assumption among Gendo, Fuyutsuki, and Seele being that, with Lilith unusable, Eva-01 is available to be stuffed with the entirety of humanity for HIP. Before Lilith crashes the party, Seele even go so far as to have Eva-01 marked with "stigmata", which seems to be their way of forcing Doors of Guf upon her. (We later see Lilith's Doors of Guf, which are orifices on the palms.) In NGE2, Eva-01's Guf capacity is all but stated, since she actually DOES receive some or all of humanity's souls, depending on the scenario. The question of course becomes, whose souls would Yui take with her?, since humanity seems to have been left behind on Earth and all. Well, there are a handful of human souls that she could probably make off with without anyone else noticing or caring. [EDIT: The Angels. I meant the Angels.] (It occurs to me that Chamber of Guf manifestation may be a latent ability of all Evas, "in the blood" as it were. It could explain their seeming propensity toward gobbling up souls, even if they already have one -- c.f. Eva-00 in episode 14 and Eva-01 in 19-20/26'.) I'm aware of the problems that "IT WILL BE VERY LONELY, BUT IF ONLY I LIVE ON, HUMANITY WILL ALWAYS BE REMEMBERED" poses. I suppose I hand wave it by mentally substituting a Seed of Life for Yui and FAR for humanity and seeing how much of that "reason why mommy is leaving forever" scene still works. Instead of being discouraged, I just manage to find my version more poignant and appealing. Oh well.
